name: Chevening Scholarship
description: The UK government's flagship international scholarship for outstanding emerging leaders to pursue a one-year master's degree in the UK.

name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
description: One of the most prestigious international scholarships, funding postgraduate study at the University of Cambridge for students who want to improve the lives of others.

name: DAAD Scholarship
description: The world's largest funding organization for international student exchange, offering scholarships for Masters and PhD studies at top German universities.

name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
description: Supports academically talented yet economically disadvantaged young Africans to access quality university education and become transformative leaders.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters
description: Fully funded Masters scholarships to study across multiple European universities. Covers tuition, travel, living expenses, and health insurance.

name: Korean Government Scholarship (KGSP)
description: Fully funded scholarship for international students to pursue undergraduate, Masters, or PhD studies in South Korea including tuition, living allowance, and airfare.

name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
description: Prestigious US government scholarship for graduate students and young professionals from over 160 countries to study and conduct research in the United States.

name: Australia Awards Scholarships
description: Prestigious international scholarships funded by the Australian government for students from partner countries to undertake full-time undergraduate or postgraduate study.

name: ETH Zurich Excellence Masters Scholarship
description: Covers living and study expenses (CHF 11,000 per semester) plus tuition fee waiver for outstanding students pursuing a Masters degree at ETH Zurich.

name: Chinese Government Scholarship
description: Fully funded Masters and PhD scholarships at over 280 Chinese universities, covering tuition, accommodation, health insurance, and a monthly living allowance.

name: Neurodiversity Alliance Scholarship
description: The largest national scholarship program investing directly in neurodivergent students with ADHD, Autism, Dyslexia, or processing disorders. Accepts both written and video story submissions.

name: ADHD Scholarship Award
description: Annual scholarship award for students diagnosed with ADHD who demonstrate academic achievement and leadership despite the challenges of their condition.

name: Jeannette Rankin Women's Scholarship
description: Supports low-income women aged 35 and older who are pursuing education for the first time or returning to school to advance their careers.

name: Adult Learner Scholarship
description: Specifically designed for adult learners over 25 returning to education. Focuses on financial need and career goals rather than traditional academic records.

name: Be Bold No-Essay Scholarship
description: A $25,000 no-essay scholarship awarded to the applicant with the boldest profile. Open to all students including adult learners and graduate students.

name: Scholarships360 No-Essay Scholarship
description: $10,000 no-essay scholarship open to all students — high school seniors, undergraduates, graduate students, and adult learners. No essay required.

name: Commonwealth Masters Scholarship
description: For students from low and middle income Commonwealth countries including Kenya to pursue a Masters degree at a UK university. Covers tuition, flights, and living costs.

name: Rotary Peace Fellowship
description: Funds up to 50 fellowships per year for Masters degrees and 12 for professional development certificates for leaders committed to peace and conflict prevention.

name: Turkiye Burslari Scholarship
description: Turkish government scholarship for international students covering tuition, accommodation, monthly stipend, health insurance, and Turkish language training.

name: Ashinaga Africa Initiative
description: Supports orphaned and vulnerable African youth to access world-class university education in Japan or the US, with full financial support and mentorship.
